mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically lifting present valuations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid acceleration in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor demand and impending earnings revisions downward, triggering immediate risk-off positioning that depresses S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal's predictive power vanishes after controlling for VIX changes.

mechanism_narrative: The joint contraction of real GDP growth and residential housing starts indicates a synchronized drag on aggregate demand, which compresses corporate revenue expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression controlling for FEDFUNDS and INDPRO over a 5-year out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects expanding capacity utilization and healthy supply-side momentum, which supports corporate margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns, but only when operating within a stable inflation environment.
failure_condition: The positive correlation between INDPRO and forward SPX returns flips to negative during periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds a 3.5% annualized growth rate.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real personal consumption expenditures (proxied by RSAFS) directly tracks consumer spending power, which constitutes the largest component of corporate revenues, thereby generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Rank IC drops below 0.02 across three consecutive rolling 3-year windows, indicating the series has lost its leading indicator status for equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index breaches a 3.0% year-over-year inflation threshold, real yields spike and central bank policy constraints bind, compressing equity valuations and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Forward SPX returns remain positive or statistically indistinguishable from zero when CPIAUCSL exceeds the 3.0% threshold, or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark in 60% of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous rise in nonfarm payrolls and decline in the unemployment rate confirms a self-reinforcing labor market recovery, which boosts consumer confidence and corporate hiring plans, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative or zero predictive rank correlation over a 10-year walk-forward test, or the interaction effect is fully absorbed by a single-factor model using only PAYEMS.

mechanism_narrative: In a housing downturn regime, the combination of collapsing housing starts and falling industrial production signals a systemic credit contraction that severely impairs corporate balance sheets, driving strongly negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of instances where both HOUST and INDPRO decline simultaneously, or the signal's Sharpe ratio falls below 0.1 over a 5-year out-of-sample evaluation.
